---
title: "California Housing Dashboard"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: bootstrap
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(flexdashboard)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)

housing <- read.csv("../Input/housing.csv")
```

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Homes by Ocean Proximity

```{r}
proximity_counts <- housing %>%
  count(ocean_proximity, name = "homes") %>%
  arrange(desc(homes))

ggplot(proximity_counts, aes(x = reorder(ocean_proximity, -homes), y = homes, fill = ocean_proximity)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(homes)), vjust = -0.4, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = "Ocean Proximity", y = "Number of Homes") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Population vs. Households

```{r}
ggplot(housing, aes(x = households, y = population)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "#2c7fb8") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(x = "Households", y = "Population") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

### Total Rooms vs. Total Bedrooms

```{r}
ggplot(housing, aes(x = total_bedrooms, y = total_rooms)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "#31a354") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(x = "Total Bedrooms", y = "Total Rooms") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```
